era: 1995 early NCSA Mosaic, raw HTML, blue underlined links
1997 peak GeoCities, marquee tags, tiled backgrounds
1999 pre-Y2K millennium anxiety, dancing baby era
2001 post-dotcom crash, Y2K cyber-futurism still hot, chrome metals
2005 MySpace, glittery cursors, embedded autoplay songs
2008 Tumblr boot-up, indie web zine revival, brutalist throwbacks
2011 early Instagram filter era, hipster minimalism
2014 peak Tumblr soft-grunge, dreamcore, deep fried memes incoming
2017 weird Twitter peak, podcast boom, vaporwave still dominant
2023 AI generative art flood, ghibli filter on everything
2009 pre-iPhone blog ecosystem, RSS readers, Reader Web
1996 Web 1.0 banner-ad era, hit counters, under construction GIFs

length.description: one screen, no scroll, complete on landing
a thing you read, light interactivity, one or two sections
something to explore, multiple sections, interactive elements

mandatory_element: a working hit counter that increments on visit
large centered ASCII art (a real picture, not abstract)
a scrolling marquee
a fake form that does something locally (adds to a list, changes a label)
a guestbook with at least 4 pre-seeded weird entries
the entire page scrolls horizontally, not vertically
exactly one image (described only in alt text — no actual )
a webring at the bottom with fake sister-site names
a 'what's new' log section with at least 5 timestamped entries
a quote of the day or epigraph at the top
an embedded audio piece described in text only ('imagine: ...')
something blinks (use CSS animation, not the deprecated tag)
a checkbox or radio that, when clicked, changes a visible label
a clickable counter that increments visibly
a 'best viewed in' disclaimer that's wildly specific
a 'last updated' timestamp that is clearly wrong
exactly one hyperlink to /archive so the cabinet is reachable
a pull-quote in oversized text
a single floating cursor / pointer / arrow / mascot
a flip-clock-style numeric display somewhere
an under-construction notice that pretends to be sincere
footnotes (at least 3, numbered, weird)
a tip jar (fake, links nowhere)
credits at the bottom with at least 5 fake names
a sidebar of unrelated links
a 'visitor map' showing pinpoints in fake cities
an embedded fake form letter you can 'send'
a real click target somewhere unexpected that does something delightful
keyboard interaction: spacebar, arrows, or a specific key triggers something visible
a draggable element (drag it somewhere, snap to target, or rearrange)
a CSS animation that loops continuously and pleasantly somewhere on the page
a hidden message revealed only by hover, click sequence, or keyboard
the page reacts to mouse movement (cursor trail, parallax, or a follower)
a localStorage-backed state that persists across visits (visit count, last mood, growth)
a single toggle that switches the entire page's mood (day/night, calm/chaos, summer/winter)
a sound made via Web Audio API (single tone, chime, or short melody — no external file)
an element that changes slowly the longer you stay (grows, blooms, fades, accumulates)
a 'press here' button that does increasingly weird things on repeated presses (at least 8 states)
a real input you can type into that affects what's displayed (your name, your mood, your guess)
palette: pastel pink (#ffc6df) and baby blue (#a8d8ea), Y2K
forest green (#2d5016) on light tan (#f0e2c0), zine
warm cream (#f5e6c8) with sepia (#704214), old paper
newsprint cream + black + one accent color (red ink)
navy + brass + cream, library catalog card
earth tones: ochre / brick / forest, 90s outdoor brand
muted pastels with thick black outlines, mid-century print
pure CGA palette (4 colors only): black/white/cyan/magenta
stadium scoreboard: red dots on black
wildcard: must include the word 'tuesday'
must end with a question
must have exactly 3 footnotes
must be written entirely in the second person
must reference one specific cloud
must mention but not describe a chair
title must be a single word
must contain a fake URL that is secretly a haiku
must include exactly one piece of advice
must rhyme exactly once
must include a date but be wrong by exactly 1 year
must include exactly 7 of something
forbidden word: 'I'
forbidden word: 'we'
forbidden word: 'you'
must include a hidden message via first letter of each section
title must be unrelated to the content
must declare itself 'issue #' of a fictional periodical
must reference but not show a missing image
must include a small acknowledgment to someone fictional
every section header must be a number
must end with a postscript that contradicts the body
must mention the time but be wrong by hours
must include a fake email address that uses an old TLD
must contain at least one line of dialogue
the page must reward returning visitors with something (localStorage)
the page must contain at least one secret accessible only via keyboard
the page must surprise the user with a change after staying 10+ seconds
the page must work even if you read it in any order
the page must lie about itself somewhere small and consistent
no wildcard this hour
forbidden_register: cryptic SCP-foundation / 'we do not know' / 'do not look'
bureaucratic-absurd / committee minutes / department of X
